nodes.label: AI as writing assistant for alternative phrasing and recommendations
nodes.label_negative: AI as unreliable or inappropriate for clinical language
nodes.grounding_span: I often use AI to help me find alternative ways of saying things and to help me formulate recommendations

nodes.label: AI as time-saving tool under time pressure
nodes.grounding_span: Most of the time Im under a time crunch and I dont have the time to spend reinventing the wheel

nodes.label: AI as source of curated recommendations
nodes.label_negative: AI as providing irrelevant or unusable recommendations
nodes.grounding_span: I usually end up picking and choosing which ones to use based on the client

nodes.label: AI as future synthesis tool for data and background info
nodes.label_negative: AI as unable to handle sensitive health data ethically
nodes.grounding_span: I'd love to be able to provide data and background info and have it spit out a summary

nodes.label: Clinical judgment as human domain
nodes.label_negative: AI involvement in final diagnostic decisions
nodes.grounding_span: The final decision on diagnoses should be mine

nodes.label: AI as skill-enhancer expanding repertoire
nodes.label_negative: AI as skill-replacer leading to atrophy
nodes.grounding_span: Hopefully, I then can take it and incorporate it into future reports

nodes.label: Efficiency and time savings
nodes.grounding_span: save so much time and mental energy

nodes.label: Clinical accuracy and ethical responsibility
nodes.grounding_span: I'll be on the lookout for ways to do this ethically

nodes.label: Professional competence and skill maintenance
nodes.grounding_span: I think sometimes it can be helpful to be provided with a way to phrase things

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI providing usable recommendations
nodes.grounding_span: I was pleasantly surprised to have a list of items I could use

nodes.label: Openness to future AI use for synthesis
nodes.grounding_span: I'd love to be able to provide data and background info and have it spit out a summary

nodes.label: Reservation about AI in clinical decision-making
nodes.grounding_span: The final decision on diagnoses should be mine

nodes.label: Pragmatic curation — selects and adapts AI outputs to context
nodes.grounding_span: I usually end up picking and choosing which ones to use based on the client

nodes.label: Ethical boundary-setting — distinguishes tasks appropriate for AI
nodes.grounding_span: this information is health data, so I can't just imput everything into an AI model
